title: UM Workflow Definition
description: VKAS artifact type defining states, transitions, guards, and routing rules for a Utilization Management workflow.

workflow_id.description: Stable identifier for this workflow type, e.g. wf_ortho_standard_ma

version.description: Semantic version of this workflow definition, e.g. 1.2.0

lob.description: Line of business this workflow applies to

display_name.description: Human-readable name for UI and audit purposes

initial_state.description: id of the state where new cases begin

id.description: Unique state identifier within this workflow

sla_ref.description: Reference to a clock-profile clock id that governs this state's deadline

terminal.description: If true, no transitions may leave this state

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for module-specific extension

from.description: State id this transition departs from

to.description: State id this transition arrives at

on.description: AsyncAPI channel message name (event) that triggers this transition, e.g. sim.case.lifecycle/CaseStateChanged

guard.description: CEL expression evaluated against the event payload. Transition fires only when true.

items.description: Action name to execute on successful transition, e.g. notify_reviewer, start_clock

actions.description: Ordered list of side-effect action names invoked after state entry

routing_rules.description: Work-routing rules evaluated in order; first match wins

condition.description: CEL expression evaluated against case context. e.g. case.urgency == 'expedited' && case.lob == 'MA'

queue.description: Work-queue identifier to route the case to

skill_tags.description: Required reviewer skill tags for queue assignment

priority_boost.description: Numeric priority adjustment applied to cases matching this rule

effective_date.description: Date from which this workflow version is effective

supersedes.description: Version string of the workflow definition this one supersedes
